content_body:
Once upon a time, just 2 hours north of Atlanta, a small town in the Appalachian Mountains called Blue Ridge waited to welcome you to the best mountain vacation.

Birthplace to the Appalachian Trail and the best fried apple pies, Blue Ridge is an idyllic small town bursting with gorgeous cabins in a tranquil setting. If you're contemplating a visit to escape the hustle and bustle, let us guide you through the must-see sights and activities that make Blue Ridge a standout destination.

Cabin Rentals of Georgia specializes in Blue Ridge luxury cabins so that you can rest assured your vacation rental has all the amenities on your wish list.

Getting to Blue Ridge

Blue Ridge is conveniently located just 90 miles north of Atlanta, making it an easy and scenic drive from the city. The journey takes you through the rolling hills and lush forests of North Georgia, providing a preview of the natural beauty that awaits. If you’re flying in, Hartsfield-Jackson Atlanta Airport is the closest major airport, and, from there, you can rent a car and enjoy the drive due north on I-75 and I-575 right into Blue Ridge.

Where to Stay


Cohutta Sunset

Imagine waking up in a cozy cabin surrounded by nature's beauty. Cabin Rentals of Georgia offers a variety of options, from intimate cabins for romantic getaways to expansive lodges perfect for family gatherings. Each cabin is uniquely decorated and equipped with modern amenities, ensuring a comfortable and memorable stay. Many of our cabins feature stunning mountain views, river access, hot tubs, game rooms, and fireplaces, making your stay as luxurious as it is cozy. The best part: our cabins are fully-stocked with all linens, towels, utensils, etc., so you only need to bring your personal belongings and food!

Exploring the Outdoors

Nestled in the mountains of North Georgia, Blue Ridge is a paradise for outdoor enthusiasts. With hundreds of miles of hiking trails, including a portion of the famous Appalachian Trail and Benton-MacKaye Trail, there’s no shortage of opportunities to explore the natural beauty of the area. Popular hikes include The Swinging Bridge, Brasstown Bald, Fall Branch Falls, and more sprinkled throughout the Appalachian Mountains.

For water activities, Lake Blue Ridge is a must-visit. This 3,290-acre lake is perfect for boating, fishing, and swimming. Rent a pontoon boat or kayak from Blue Ridge Boat Rentals and spend a relaxing day on the water, or enjoy a picnic on the shore. The Toccoa River, flowing through Blue Ridge from south to north, is also popular for fly fishing and tubing/kayaking.

Discovering Downtown Blue Ridge

Downtown Blue Ridge is a delightful blend of old and new, with historic buildings housing a variety of shops, restaurants, and galleries. Stroll down Main Street to find everything from antiques and handmade crafts to trendy boutiques and specialty stores. Stop by Huck’s General Store for nostalgic candy, toys, and unique gifts or Blue Ridge Beauty Barn for the best array of non-toxic beauty products housed in one of town's oldest buildings.

When it comes to dining, Blue Ridge does not disappoint. With a variety of casual cafes and fine dining, there’s something to satisfy every palate. Ferraro's Kitchen is a popular choice, offering Italian dishes in an elevated setting. For a more casual meal, try the Rum Cake Lady Cuban cafe, where you can enjoy authentic Cuban cuisine in either their Blue Ridge or McCaysville location.

Seasonal Activities and Events


Courtesy Blue Ridge Mountains

Blue Ridge is a year-round destination, and each season offers unique attractions and activities. Spring and summer bring vibrant wildflowers and lush greenery, perfect for hiking, biking, and exploring waterfalls like Fall Branch Falls and Sea Creek Falls.

Fall is perhaps the most popular time to visit Blue Ridge, thanks to the stunning display of autumn foliage. The mountains are ablaze with reds, oranges, and yellows, creating a icturesque backdrop for all your outdoor adventures. This is also the time for apple picking at local orchards and enjoying festivals like Fall Arts in the Park or the Georgia Apple Festival in nearby Ellijay, GA.

Winter in Blue Ridge is magical, with the possibility of snow adding an extra touch of charm to the landscape. The colder months are perfect for cozying up in your cabin by the fire, but there are also plenty of activities to enjoy. Downtown Blue Ridge offers ice skating, and the Blue Ridge Scenic Railway runs a special Santa Express train ride that’s a hit with families.

Cultural and Historical Attractions


Courtesy Blue Ridge Mountains

Blue Ridge boasts a rich history and vibrant arts scene. Start at the Blue Ridge Mountains Arts Association, offering rotating exhibits, workshops, and events showcasing local artists' work. The historic Blue Ridge Depot, built in 1905, is home to the Blue Ridge Scenic Railway, offering scenic train rides along the Toccoa River. The historic downtown area itself is a testament to Blue Ridge’s history, with many buildings dating back to the early 20th century.

If you're curious about the lore of Bigfoot, North Georgia is the place for you! The Expedition: Bigfoot! Museum is just 10 minutes from downtown Blue Ridge, boasting an enjoyable array of artifacts, history, and the nation's largest footprint casts.

Tips for Planning Your Georgia Mountain Vacation


Blue Ridge Lake Sanctuary

  • Plan Ahead: Blue Ridge is a popular destination, especially during peak seasons like fall and summer. Book your luxury cabin and activities well in advance to ensure availability.
  • Pack Accordingly: The weather in Blue Ridge can bring some surprises, especially in the mountains. Pack accordingly and be prepared for a slight in conditions, whether hiking, boating, or exploring downtown.
  • Respect Nature: Blue Ridge is known for its pristine natural beauty, and it’s important to help keep it that way. Follow Leave No Trace principles when hiking, and be mindful of local wildlife.

 

Whether you’re seeking adventure, relaxation, or a bit of both, Blue Ridge, GA is the perfect destination. With its breathtaking landscapes, charming downtown, and endless opportunities for outdoor fun, it’s no wonder so many people fall in love with this mountain town.

So pack your bags, leave your worries behind, and get ready to experience the beauty of North Georgia.

Reserve one of our luxury cabins online with the "Instant Quote" button or give our Blue Ridge vacation specialists a call at (706) 432-2140 today!
title: So, You Want to Visit Blue Ridge...
